name: override_detection_and_confirmation
description: Warm day, HVAC in cool mode at comfort_cool (74F). User bumps thermostat to heat mode at 14:00. After 10-minute confirm period passes (14:10), override is confirmed. Classification cycle at 14:30 is suppressed by active override.
notes: Tests the full override detection → confirmation → classification suppression lifecycle.
Issue #476: migrated to use_coordinator=true, skip_startup_coalesce=true. thermostat_state_changed
dispatches via fake_hass.states.async_set() through the real _async_thermostat_changed listener;
the 14:10 temp_update event forces scheduler.advance_to() past the real override_confirm_seconds=600
async_call_later timer, letting it genuinely fire.
The 14:30 assertion was 'no_action' in the legacy-simulator version. apply_classification() returns
immediately when _manual_override_active is True (automation.py:1265-1271) WITHOUT emitting any
event, so the correct outcome is the carried-forward previous decision (override_confirmed from
14:10), not a literal 'no_action' string outcomes.py has no sentinel for.
manual_grace_seconds set to 3600 (1 hour) explicitly: the harness default (900s/15min, from
build_engine.py's _DEFAULT_CONFIG) auto-clears the override via real grace expiry at 14:25 —
BEFORE this scenario's 14:30 classification event — contradicting the scenario's own stated intent
('Classification cycle at 14:30 is suppressed by ACTIVE override'). The legacy simulator never
modeled the grace timer here, so this premise gap was invisible until real timers ran. Extending
the grace window keeps the override genuinely active through 14:30, matching the designed narrative.
14:30's classification hvac_mode was changed from 'cool' (same as 13:01) to 'off': a first revert
test with the ORIGINAL identical classification passed even with the override-suppression guard
(automation.py:1265-1271) fully disabled — because apply_classification()'s SEPARATE
day_type/hvac_mode dedup (automation.py:1355-1356) coincidentally produced the same 'no new
decision' outcome regardless of whether suppression genuinely worked, making the assertion
vacuous. Using a different hvac_mode at 14:30 makes the two mechanisms distinguishable: with the
guard disabled, a genuine NEW classification_applied fires (confirmed); with it restored, nothing
new fires and the outcome correctly stays override_confirmed. git diff confirmed clean after both
revert-test passes.

events.note: Warm summer afternoon. Indoor at comfort_cool=74F. HVAC holding cool mode.
Warm-day classification: HVAC cool at comfort_cool=74F.
User bumps thermostat to heat mode. Override detection fires. 10-minute confirm window starts.
10 minutes have elapsed. Thermostat still in heat mode → override confirmed.

Scheduled 30-min classification cycle — deliberately a DIFFERENT hvac_mode (off, not cool) from the 13:01 classification, so that if suppression were bypassed a genuinely NEW classification_applied event would fire (breaking the separate day_type/hvac_mode dedup in automation.py:1355-1356) — making the 14:30 assertion below actually discriminating rather than passing vacuously regardless of whether the override guard works. Override is active — should be suppressed.

assertions.reason: Initial warm-day classification applied: hvac_mode=cool, target=comfort_cool=74F.
Thermostat state changed to heat mode while classification says cool — override detection fires.
assertions.note: Override detection fires in coordinator _async_thermostat_changed listener.

assertions.reason: 10 minutes elapsed with thermostat still in heat mode → override_confirm_seconds=600 expired → override confirmed.
assertions.note: Confirm timer is an async_call_later callback in the coordinator layer.

assertions.reason: Classification cycle while manual_override_active=True: apply_classification() returns immediately (automation.py:1265-1271) without emitting any event, so the outcome stays the carried-forward override_confirmed from 14:10 — proving the classification cycle was genuinely suppressed. Corrected from legacy 'no_action' — see notes.

verdict.summary: Override detected on thermostat mode change, confirmed after 10-min delay, suppresses subsequent classification
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (cool, 74F) → override_detected (14:00) → override_confirmed (14:10) → classification cycle suppressed at 14:30 (no new decision)
verdict.expected_behavior: CA respects user's manual override and stops re-applying classification until override clears
